patches_applied.rule_applied: Rule 32 — treaty_posture list of treaties relevant to active conflicts

patches_actually_applied.material_change_justification: Five new escalation events documented this cycle: Russia deadliest civilian strike wave 2026, Sudan SAF Blue Nile offensive, Sudan SAF gold mine strikes, Shoigu Article 51 warning, Houthi Red Sea intervention warning. All meet strategic significance threshold.

new_value.event: Russia conducted deadliest civilian strike wave of 2026, killing 73 civilians and injuring over 400 across Zaporizhia, Kramatorsk, Dnipro, Merefa, and Sumy in four-day pre-ceasefire campaign (4-8 May)
new_value.strategic_significance: Demonstrates structural pattern of ceasefire-adjacent civilian targeting as pre-ceasefire bargaining chip; second documented cycle (Easter + Victory Day) approaching structural threshold

new_value.event: Sudan Armed Forces captured Damazin in Blue Nile state, opening new front threatening north-south partition
new_value.strategic_significance: Structural shift from year-long stalemate to multi-domain offensive operations; opens potential permanent failed-state corridor from Khartoum to Ethiopian border

new_value.event: Sudan Armed Forces conducted manned airstrikes on RSF-controlled gold mines in Kordofan as economic warfare
new_value.strategic_significance: Introduction of economic warfare targeting signals shift from purely kinetic to hybrid strategy; aims to degrade RSF revenue streams via UAE networks

new_value.event: Security Council Secretary Sergei Shoigu warned Finland and Baltic states over alleged Ukrainian use of their airspace, invoking Article 51 of UN Charter
new_value.strategic_significance: Novel escalation in legal framing against NATO members; creates legal predicate for future kinetic action that could be framed as defensive rather than offensive

new_value.event: Houthis issued military intervention warning threatening Red Sea chokepoint disruption if Iran attacked
new_value.strategic_significance: Creates dual-chokepoint risk (Hormuz + Bab al-Mandeb) threatening 30 percent of global seaborne oil trade and 10 percent of all maritime commerce

patches_actually_applied.rule_applied: Rule 30 — f_flag_history additive log with status updates
patches_actually_applied.material_change_justification: No new F-flags applied this cycle. All existing F4 and F3 flags maintained per reasoner analysis. No status changes to Resolved.

new_value.detail: Ceasefire fragile with daily violations and no Phase 2 framework; Hamas rejection of disarmament unverified
Switzerland talks produced protocols but fighting continues; talks interim (AMBER, not RED expulsion)
Impasse confirmed; no verification of decouple proposal progress; impasse is AMBER-equivalent (talks stalled, not expelled)
No new data contradicting limited framing inconsistency; Op Eternal Darkness scale persists
Ongoing Hormuz closure and Gulf attacks contradict dismantled claim

patches_actually_applied.material_change_justification: Material updates to mediation architecture for Russia-Ukraine (US bilateral ceasefire collapsed), US-Israel-Iran (nuclear talks at impasse, decouple proposal rejected), Sudan (Quad mediation collapsed), and DRC (Switzerland talks produced protocols but fighting continues).

new_value.un_security_council: Deadlocked — Russia veto power blocks substantive resolutions; no active UNSC mediation framework
Deadlocked — US veto blocks ceasefire enforcement resolutions; humanitarian resolutions pass but lack enforcement
No active UNSC mediation; UNIFIL mandate renewal pending

frameworks: Nuclear talks (at impasse; decouple proposal rejected 27 April 2026)
new_value.un_security_council: Deadlocked — no UNSC role in nuclear talks; regional mediation absent
No active UNSC mediation; Quad mediation (US, UAE, Saudi Arabia, Egypt) collapsed

frameworks: Switzerland talks (April 2026 protocols produced but fighting continues)
new_value.un_security_council: No active UNSC mediation; regional actors (Rwanda, Uganda) not bound by protocols
Deadlocked — China and Russia block substantive resolutions

patches_actually_applied.rule_applied: Rule 32 — treaty_posture list of treaties relevant to active conflicts
patches_actually_applied.material_change_justification: No material treaty status changes this cycle. New START remains suspended. NPT compliance contested for DPRK (7 tests 2026) and Iran (nuclear impasse). CTBT not in force but DPRK tests violate norms.

new_value.details: Russia suspended participation February 2023; no successor negotiations active; absence of verification framework leaves no transparency on nuclear posture changes
DPRK nuclear acceleration continues (7 tests 2026 to April); Iran nuclear programme at impasse; NPT compliance verification relies on IAEA safeguards, absent for DPRK and contested for Iran
DPRK not a signatory; 7 tests 2026 to April violate CTBT norms but treaty not in force; no enforcement mechanism
